conbody.p: Overview: The Triage Overview dashboard enables CDI Managers to collect important metrics, including the count of prioritized and reviewed encounters, initial review time, and instances of missed concurrent and top-ranked reviews. This helps to measure how well encounter prioritization is working and assess the productivity of clinical documentation specialists. The dashboard also helps you group data by week, month, quarter, and year and analyze trends over different time periods and analyze them more easily.
By default, the report displays data for last 3 calendar months based on the admission date.
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE Triage Triage and CDS Assistant Usage Triage Overview .

Triage Overview dashboard displays the following:
KPI Cards: Encounters Ranked, Ranked Encounters Reviewed, Avg. Review TAT (Hours), Missed Top Ranked Reviews.
Encounters Ranked
% Ranked Encounters Reviewed
Total Reviews
Avg. Review TAT (Hours)
Missed Concurrent Reviews
Missed Top Ranked Reviews

ul.li: Encounters that have been assigned a rank in CDE Triage are considered as ranked encounters. The current rank of the encounter may be empty or not specified.

li.p: Encounters are ranked based only according to the rules specified for prioritization within CDE Triage. Any encounter that does not meet the criteria is categorized as an unranked encounter.

ul.li:
The following encounter types are considered:
Both ranked (standard and custom) and unranked encounters
Highest rank of the encounter
Data for ‘Review Not Needed’ encounters is excluded.

conbody.p: On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets filtered in the charts.
For View By option, refer .
For filters on all pages, refer .
For Footnotes, refer .

entry.p: Displays the count and percentage of encounters ranked for the selected time period.

Format: Encounters Ranked (% Encounters Ranked) 76 (77%).
Encounters Ranked: Count of encounters that were ranked based on the rules.
% of Encounters Ranked: Out of total encounters (admissions/discharges), the percentage of encounters that were ranked for the selected time frame.
% Encounters Ranked = (Count of encounters ranked/ Total Encounters) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.Total Encounters could be total admissions or total discharges.
Displays the count and percentage of ranked encounters reviewed for the selected time period.
Format: Ranked Encounters Reviewed (% Ranked Encounters Reviewed) 67 (89%).

ul.li: Ranked Encounters Reviewed: Count of all ranked encounters (standard and custom) that have an initial review date/time assigned to it.
% Ranked Encounters Reviewed: (Count of ranked encounters reviewed /Count of encounters ranked) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.

entry.p: Displays the average Review TAT in hours for the selected time period, i.e. the number of hours between Review Complete Date/Time and when the Working Review was first created for the selected time period.
Review TAT (Hours) for each encounter = ‘Review Complete’ (Date/Time stamp) – Initial Review (Date/Time stamp).
Avg. Review TAT (Hours) = Sum of Review TAT (Hours) of all the reviewed encounters / Encounters Reviewed.
entry.note: 'Review Complete' (Date/Time stamp) is recorded when the CDS selects the encounter status as 'Review Complete' and saves the encounter.

entry.p: Displays the count of encounters with the highest rank as 1, 2, or 6 that were not reviewed by the CDS. These encounters do not have an initial review date/time. The encounters may or may not have a Discharge Date/Time assigned.
entry.note: Only top ranked (standard highest rank 1, 2 and 6) encounters are considered.
entry.p: Missed Top Rank Reviews = Count of top-ranked encounters (Rank 1, 2, and 6) that do not have an initial review, i.e. where the Initial Review Date/Time is either empty or not specified.

conbody.p: This chart allows users to analyze count and percentage of prioritized encounters for the selected "View By" option.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Includes encounters other than 'Review Not Needed' that have ever been assigned a standard or custom rank in CDE Triage. The visual shows the data for all standard and custom ranks by default. For data on encounters with specific ranks, use the 'Highest Rank’ slicer.

row.entry:
Displays the total count of admissions or discharges for the selected View By option. The total encounters are the count of encounters that have an admission date/time or discharge date/time assigned to them.
The count of total encounters should match with total discharges on the Executive Overview dashboard for the same date range.

entry.p: Encounters Ranked - Displays the count of encounters that were ranked (based on the rules).

ul.li: All ranks (Standard and Custom ranks) are considered.
Highest Rank of the encounter is considered.

entry.p: Unranked Encounters - Out of the total discharges, the count of encounters that were not ranked for the selected View By option.
Encounters that have ever been assigned a rank in CDE Triage are considered as Ranked Encounters. The current rank of the encounter may be ‘Blank’ as well.
% Unranked Encounters = (Count of encounters NOT ranked {Unranked Encounters}/ Total Encounters (Admissions/Discharges)) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.
entry.note: Encounters are ranked only based on the rules that are defined for the prioritization within CDE Triage and if any encounter that does not fall under the criteria is defined as an Unranked Encounter.

entry.p: Out of the total discharges/admissions, percentage of encounters that were ranked (based on the rules) for the selected View By option.
% Encounters Ranked = (Count of encounters ranked / Total Encounters (Admissions/Discharges)) X100. The value is rounded to 0 decimal places.

conbody.p: This chart allows users to analyze the review rate of the prioritized encounters for the selected "View By" option.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Includes encounters other than 'Review Not Needed' that have ever been assigned a standard or custom rank in CDE Triage. The visual shows the data for all standard and custom ranks by default. For data on encounters with specific ranks, use the 'Highest Rank’ slicer.

row.entry:
Displays the percentage of the ranked encounters reviewed for the selected "View By" option, out of the total ranked encounters.
% Ranked Encounters Reviewed = (Count of ranked encounters reviewed /Count of encounters ranked) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.
Count of ranked encounters reviewed = Count of all the ranked encounters with an initial review date/time.

entry.p: Ranked Encounters Reviewed - Count of all ranked encounters that have an initial review date/time assigned to it.

Unranked Encounters Reviewed - Count of all unranked encounters that have an initial review date/time assigned to it. Encounters are ranked only based on the rules that are defined for the prioritization within CDE Triage and if any encounter that does not fall under the criteria is defined as an Unranked Encounter.
% Unranked Encounters Reviewed - Out of all unranked encounters, this metric displays the percentage of the unranked encounters reviewed.
% Unranked Encounters Reviewed = (Count of unranked encounters reviewed /Count of encounters unranked) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.

conbody.p: This chart enables report users to analyze and compare the breakdown of ranked and unranked initial reviews for the selected "View By" option. This helps the CDS managers mainly to analyse CDS working on unranked encounters, instead of ranked encounters.This chart does not change as per View By options selected.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Ranked Initial Reviews: Includes encounters other than 'Review Not Needed' that have ever been assigned a standard or custom rank in CDE Triage. The metric shows the data for all standard and custom ranks by default. For data on encounters with specific ranks, use the 'Highest Rank’ slicer.

row.entry:
Displays the count and percentage of ranked encounters reviewed for the selected time period.
Ranked Initial Reviews: Count of all ranked encounters (both standard and custom ranks) that have an initial review date/time assigned to it.
% Ranked Initial Reviews: ({Count of ranked encounters with an initial review date/time} /Total Count of Initial Reviews) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.

Total Initial Reviews: Count of all encounters reviewed i,e count of all encounters with an initial review date/time. This displays the total count of all encounters (both ranked and unranked) for the selected time period.

Displays the count and percentage of unranked encounters reviewed for the selected time period.
Unranked Initial Reviews: This count represents all encounters, both standard and custom ranks, that have an initial review date/time assigned to them.
% Unranked Initial Reviews: ({Count of unranked encounters with an initial review date/time} /Total Count of Initial Reviews) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.

conbody.p: This chart shows the average review completion time (in hours) for each encounter based on the selected "View By" option. It helps CDS managers track productivity trends over time and identify any changes in the CDS process.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Includes encounters other than 'Review Not Needed'. The visual shows the data for all ranked (standard/custom) and unranked encounters by default. For data on encounters with specific ranks, use the 'Highest Rank’ slicer.

row.entry:
Displays the average Review Turnaround Time (TAT) in hours for the selected "View By" option. Review TAT represents the time taken by the CDS to complete a review for an encounter, calculated as the number of hours between the Review Complete (Date/Time) and the initial creation of the Working Review.
Review TAT (Hours) for each encounter = ‘Review Complete’ (Date/Time) – Initial Review (Date/Time).
Avg. Review TAT (Hours) = (Sum of Review TAT (Hours) of all the reviewed encounters / Encounters Reviewed).
'Review Complete' (Date/Time) is recorded when the CDS selects the encounter status as 'Review Complete' and saves the encounter.
Latest/most recent Review Complete' (Date/Time) for an encounter is considered.

conbody.p: This chart helps users examine cases that were discharged without CDS review or were reviewed after discharge, missing a chance for documentation improvement.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Includes encounters other than 'Review Not Needed'. The visual shows the data for all ranked (standard/custom) and unranked encounters by default. For data on encounters with specific ranks, use the 'Highest Rank’ slicer.

row.entry:
Displays the count of encounters for which review was performed after discharge, i.e. out of total encounters discharged, this metric displays the count of encounters with no initial review or initial review after discharge for the selected View By option.
Missed Concurrent Reviews = Discharged encounters with no initial review or initial review after discharge, i.e. Initial Review Date/Time is Blank or Initial Review Date/Time > Discharge Date/Time.

conbody.p: This chart allows report users to analyze the top rank encounters (Rank 1, 2, and 6) that are not reviewed by the CDS and therefore missed an opportunity for documentation improvement.
Info Icon(Text on hover): Includes encounters other than 'Review Not Needed' that have ever been assigned a standard Highest Rank of 1, 2, or 6 in CDE Triage.

row.entry:
Displays the count of encounters with the top rank as 1, 2, or 6 that were not reviewed by the CDS. These encounters don't have an initial review date/time and may or may not have a discharge date/time.
Missed Top Rank Reviews = Count of top-ranked encounters (Rank 1, 2, and 6) without an initial review, indicated by an empty or unspecified Initial Review Date/Time.Only top ranked (Standard Highest Rank 1, 2 and 6) encounters are considered.

When this option is selected, all charts in the dashboard display the data by month of admission in ascending order. When you click this option, Group By and Week Start Day filters automatically become available for selection.
When the ‘View By’ is Admit Month, the data in all the charts is sorted by Admit month in ascending order.Admit Month is selected as the default 'View By' option.

entry.p: When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by month of discharge in ascending order. When you click this option, Group By and Week Start Day filters automatically become available for selection.
When the ‘View By’ is Discharge Month, the data in all the charts is sorted by Discharge month in ascending order.
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by facility in ascending order.
When the ‘View By’ is Facility, the data in all the charts is sorted by Facility name in ascending order.
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by payer in ascending order.
When the ‘View By’ is Payer, the data in all the charts is sorted by Payer in ascending order.

row.entry:
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by CDS.
When the ‘View By’ is CDS, the data in all the charts is sorted by CDS name in ascending order.

When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by the highest rank assigned to the encounter.
When the ‘View By’ is Highest Rank, the data in all the charts is sorted by Highest Rank in ascending order.Encounters that do not have a rank assigned are grouped under the 'Unranked' category.

li.p: Allows user to select the admit date range.
Format: Between date range, example: 4/1/2023 - 6/30/2023.

ul.li: Default: Last 3 calendar months date range selected by default.
Displays the discharge date range.

li.p: Format: Between date range, example: 4/1/2023 - 6/30/2023.

ul.li: With the Reset button, you can reset both the admit and discharge date range.
Tooltip: Ctrl + Click to reset both Admit Date and Discharge Date.
Displays both active and inactive facilities in alphabetical order that you have access to.
You can select multiple facilities.
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID)
Default: ALL
Displays both active and inactive payers in alphabetical order associated with the facilities you have access to.
You can select multiple payers.
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID)
Default: ALL

li.p: Displays both active and inactive CDSs in alphabetical order associated with the facilities user has access to.
User can select multiple CDSs
"Unassigned CDS" displays data for the encounters that do not have a CDS assigned.
Users can search the CDS by CDS ID or Name.
Format: CDS Name (CDS ID)
Default: ALL

ul.li: This filter displays the list of available units.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Unit.
Default: ALL.

li.p: Displays all standard and custom ranks that can be assigned to the encounters.
User can select multiple ranks.
Users can search the rank number.
“Unranked” displays data for all encounters that are not ranked.
Format: Rank # ( such as Rank 1, Rank 2,)
Default: ALL

entry.note: This slicer filters the data based on the highest standard/custom rank assigned to an encounter (where 1 is the highest standard rank and 11 is the lowest standard rank).

row.entry:
These options are available only when the View By option is selected as Admit Date/Discharge Date.
See .

ul.li: Displays the available patient types. Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Default: Inpatient
Displays the list of available admit service.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Admit Service.
Default: ALL
Displays the available patient types.Example: ED, IP, etc.
You can select multiple Visit Type.
You can search the Visit Type.
Default: ALL

li.p: Displays the available options for Weekend Only Stay: Y and N.
User can select more than one option.
Weekend Only Stay = Y (Unassigned encounters admitted after Friday, 6PM and Discharged before Sunday, 11.59 PM).
Default: ALL.

row.entry: The count of encounters that have ever been assigned a standard or custom rank in CDE Triage. The current rank for the encounter may be blank; % Encounters Ranked = Encounters Ranked / Total Encounters x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%. Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.

The count of encounters that have never been assigned a standard or custom rank in CDE Triage. % Unranked Encounters = Unranked Encounters / Total Encounters x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%. Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.
The count of ranked encounters reviewed with or without clarifications. % Ranked Encounters Reviewed = Ranked Encounters Reviewed / Encounters Ranked x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.

The count of unranked encounters reviewed with or without clarifications. % Unranked Encounters Reviewed = Unranked Encounters Reviewed / Encounters Ranked x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.

Avg. Review TAT (Hours) = Sum of Review TAT(Hours) of all the reviewed encounters / Encounters Reviewed (Initial Reviews); Review TAT (hours) for an encounter = (Review Complete Date/Time- Initial Review Date/Time) hours. E.g., 28 Hours.
The count of encounters that are discharged and are not reviewed by the CDS or were reviewed post discharge (Initial Review Date/Time > Discharge Date/Time).
The count of encounters with the Highest Rank as 1, 2, or 6 that were not reviewed by the CDS.
